title: UNION ALL might return error when the first child is table dual
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
create table t(a int, b int);
set @@tidb_max_chunk_size=32;
select a from (select 100 as a, 100 as b union all select * from t) t where b != 0;
```
make sure that the number of satisfied rows from t is more than 32.

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

Return the correct result.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

Error thrown `Internal error: UnionExec chunk column count mismatch, req: 2, result: 1`

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(a INT, b INT, c VARCHAR(10));
CREATE TABLE t2(x INT, y INT DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO t1(a, b, c) VALUES (1, 2, 'test'), (3, 4, NULL), (5, 6, 'example');
INSERT INTO t2(x, y) VALUES (10, 1), (20, NULL), (30, 3);
SELECT a FROM (SELECT 100 AS a, 100 AS b UNION ALL SELECT a, b FROM t1) AS subquery WHERE b IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: UNION ALL with table dual causes chunk column count mismatch
